title: Strength Training Program
description: Progressive strength training focusing on building raw power and increasing performance on major lifts through structured periodization.
overview: This program emphasizes progressive overload and compound movements to build maximum strength. Using a 5-3-1 style periodization, lifters will increase their major lift maxes while building overall strength and conditioning. Expected strength gains: 5-10% increase every 4-6 weeks.

weekly_schedule.exercises: Squats (5x3, 3x2, 1x1 pyramid) + Box Squats (3x3) + Leg Press (3x6) + GHD Sit-ups (3x10)
Bench Press (8x2 with 55%) + Incline Dumbbell (3x6) + Rows (3x5) + Push-ups (3xMax) + Tri-work (2x8)

weekly_schedule.focus: Bench press or overhead press focus
weekly_schedule.exercises: Bench Press (5x3, 3x2, 1x1 pyramid) + Close Grip (3x4) + Machine Rows (3x6) + Dips (3x5)
Deadlifts (8x2 with 55%) + Front Squats (3x4) + Leg Curl (3x6) + Ab Wheel (3x8)
Focus on weak points: Rack Pulls, Pin Presses, Board Presses, Plate Squats

nutrition.content: 4 whole eggs + 2 slices whole wheat toast + 1 banana
50g whey protein + 50g oats + 1 banana + 240ml whole milk
250g beef + 250g rice + vegetables + 1 tbsp olive oil
300g chicken or fish + 250g sweet potato + vegetables

tips: Progressive overload is everything - track every lift in a notebook
Deload week every 4 weeks at 60% intensity for recovery
Form over ego - never sacrifice form for heavier weight
Use a lifting belt and wrist wraps for heavy lifts
Prioritize sleep - 8+ hours for CNS recovery
Take 3-5 minutes rest between heavy compound sets
Video record your lifts to check form
Join a lifting community for motivation and form checks
Use RPE (Rate of Perceived Exertion) - stop 1-2 reps short of failure
Include mobility work to prevent injuries
Supplement with creatine, protein, and a multivitamin
Be consistent - missing workouts will stall progress
